data.title: Ouster Inc's Q2 revenue: $35.05 million, beats estimates. Stock surges after reporting losses at 38 cents/share.
data.description: Ouster Inc (NASDAQ: OUST) released its Q2 financial results exceeding expectations after market close. The lidar company reported a revenue of $35.05 million, surpassing estimates of $33.77 million. Loss per share was 38 cents, lower than the estimated 55 cents. OUST saw significant gains in the latest session, with a 30% year-over-year and 7% sequential increase in total revenue. They shipped 5,500 sensors in Q2. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.description: Roman Storm's journey is far from ordinary, starting with teaching himself how to code and eventually landing odd jobs in the United States upon immigrating.

data.title: Firm Capital Mortgage Investment Corp reports Q2/2025 results, declares monthly dividends for October and November.
data.description: Today, in Toronto on August 7, 2025, the Firm Capital Mortgage Investment Corporation (referred to as the “Corporation”) disclosed its financial records for the first half of the year concluding on June 30, 2025. The Corporation’s financial statements are available for public scrutiny and trading under various stock symbols including TSX FC, FC.DB.J, FC.DB.K, and FC.DB.L.

data.title: Eli Lilly's obesity pill Orforglipron shows 12% weight loss in Phase 3 trial with 3,000 obese patients, trails Novo Nordisk.
data.description: Eli Lilly and Co. saw a significant drop in stock value after releasing Phase 3 trial results for orforglipron, an investigational GLP-1 receptor agonist targeting obesity in over 3,000 patients without diabetes. All doses of orforglipron met primary and secondary endpoints at 72 weeks, with the highest dose leading to an average weight loss of 12.4% (27.3 lbs) compared to 0.9% (2.2 lbs) with placebo. This weight reduction exceeded Wall Street's 15% expectation, with 59.6% of high-dose participants losing at least 10% body weight, and 39.6% losing at least 15%. Comparatively, Novo Nordisk A/S' oral semaglutide 25 mg achieved a 16.6% weight loss. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Serve Robotics stock falls as Q2 earnings miss expectations: $0.24 loss per share vs. expected $0.21, revenue of $642,000.
data.description: After the market closed on Thursday, Serve Robotics, Inc. (NASDAQ: SERV) revealed its second-quarter financial results. The report indicated a quarterly loss of 24 cents per share, falling short of the estimated 21 cents loss. However, the company surpassed revenue expectations with $642,000 in quarterly revenue compared to the projected $624,800. For real-time updates on SERV stock performance, refer to the live stock price and chart. Visit the Benzinga.com website for further details on this announcement.

data.title: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc. prices private offering of $650 million in Senior Notes at 6.750% interest due 2034.
data.description: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc. and its subsidiaries have disclosed the pricing details of their recent offering, totaling $650 million in aggregate principal amount of 6.750% Senior Notes due 2034. The Notes, bearing interest at 6.750% per annum, are set to mature on February 15, 2034. Interest payments on the Notes will be made semi-annually on February 15 and August 15 of each year, with the first payment scheduled to begin on an upcoming date.

data.title: Strathcona Resources Ltd. released Q2 2025 financial results, declares quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share.
data.description: Calgary, Alberta – On August 7, 2025, Strathcona Resources Ltd. (TSX: SCR) released its financial and operational data for the second quarter of 2025. The company announced a quarterly dividend of $0.30 for each common share in addition to the reports.

data.title: SmartCentres REIT reports Q2 2025 results: Occupancy 98.6%, 148,000 sq ft leased, positive rent growth. CEO shares optimism.
data.description: In Toronto, SmartCentres Real Estate Investment Trust, listed as SRU.UN on the TSX, has released its financial and operational results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. CEO Mitchell Goldhar stated that following a successful Q1, the trust saw ongoing leasing demand and operational progress in Q2. Occupancy rates rose to 98.6%, with around 148,000 square feet leased during the quarter and a notable increase in rents.

data.title: Dream Office REIT reports Q2 2025 results; conference call to discuss on Aug 8, 2025 at 10 a.m. Total properties & financials shared.
data.description: Dream Office Real Estate Investment Trust (D.UN-TSX) disclosed its financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. A conference call will be held on August 8, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. (ET) to discuss these results. As of June 30, 2025, the Trust reported a total of properties, compared to on March 31, 2025, and on June 30, 2024. The figures represent unaudited data.

data.title: Transcontinental Realty Investors, Inc. reports Q2 2025 earnings: Net income of $0.2M, $0.02/share, compared to $1.5M, $0.17/share in Q2 2024; Total occupancy at 82%.
data.description: Transcontinental Realty Investors, Inc. (NYSE:TCI) has released its operational results for the quarter ending on June 30, 2025. In this period, the company reported a net income of $0.2 million attributable to common shares, equivalent to $0.02 per diluted share. This marks a decrease from the same period in 2024 when the net income was $1.5 million, or $0.17 per diluted share. The total occupancy rate as of June 30, 2025, stood at 82%, with multifamily properties showing 94% occupancy and other properties at 57%.

data.title: Income Opportunity Realty Investors, Inc. reports Q2 net income of $1.0 million, down from $1.2 million in 2024 due to decreased interest income.
data.description: Income Opportunity Realty Investors, Inc. (NYSE American: IOR) has released its financial performance for the quarter ending on June 30, 2025. During this period, the company reported a net income attributable to common shares of $1.0 million, equivalent to $0.24 per diluted share. This represents a decrease from the prior year's performance, where a net income of $1.2 million or $0.28 per share was recorded for the same quarter in 2024. The decline in net income is primarily due to a reduction in interest income. Income Opportunity Realty Investors is a prominent player in the real estate sector.

data.title: American Realty Investors, Inc. reports Q2 2025 net income of $2.8 million, $0.18 per diluted share, up from $1.2 million in 2024. Total occupancy: 82%.
data.description: Dallas-based American Realty Investors, Inc. (NYSE:ARL) has released its financial results for the quarter ending on June 30, 2025. During this period, the company recorded a net income attributable to common shares of $2.8 million, translating to $0.18 per diluted share. This marks a significant increase from the $1.2 million, or $0.07 per diluted share, reported for the same period in 2024. Key financial highlights include a total occupancy rate of 82% as of June 30, 2025, with multifamily properties boasting a strong 94% occupancy rate, while commercial properties stood at 57%.

data.title: Chartwell announced Q2 2025 results with $78.4M revenue increase, $5.7M net loss, 51.1% FFO rise, and $0.7B acquisitions. CEO expects 95% occupancy by 2025 end. Company focuses on growth and optimizing portfolio. Liquidity at $423.1M. Increases stake in The Sumach by Chartwell, acquires Chartwell Le Prescott II.
data.description: Chartwell Retirement Residences reported its results for Q2 2025, including a 41.4% increase in resident revenue and a net loss of $5.7 million. Funds from operations (FFO) rose by 51.1%. The company completed over $0.7 billion in acquisitions in 2025 so far and has another $0.6 billion in planned investments for the rest of the year. Weighted average same property occupancy increased to 91.9% from Q2 2024. The company aims to reach 95% occupancy by the end of 2025 and expects 93.5% by September. In terms of financials, FFO was $67.6 million for Q2 2025 ($0.24 per unit). For 2025 YTD, resident revenue increased by $138.1 million, and FFO was $123.7 million ($0.44 per unit). Liquidity stood at $423.1 million as of June 30, 2025, with an interest coverage ratio of 3.0. Chartwell continues to focus on growing occupancy, optimizing its portfolio, and pursuing new developments for future growth. Key recent activities include acquiring an additional 5% ownership in a Toronto residence and a 50% stake in a new residence in Prescott.

data.title: Instacart's parent company reports Q2 revenue of $914 million and EPS of 41 cents, beating estimates. Shares rise.
data.description: Maplebear Inc, the parent company of Instacart (NASDAQ: CART), released its second quarter financial results after the market closed on Thursday. The company reported Q2 revenue of $914 million, surpassing estimates of $896.91 million. Additionally, Q2 EPS was reported at 41 cents, exceeding estimates of 39 cents. Instacart saw a 17% increase in orders year-over-year, totaling 82.7 million in the second quarter. Overall, total revenue rose by 11%. CART's stock showed notable gains in the latest trading session. More details can be found on the Benzinga website.

data.title: Plaza Retail REIT reports Q2 2025 financial results: Revenues up 3.6% to $31,791k, NOI up 3.8% to $19,098k, profit up 419.2% to $12,653k. FFO increased 5.3% to $11,187k, FFO per unit up 5.3% to $0.100, AFFO decreased 15.8% to $7,511k, AFFO per unit down 16.3% to $0.067. Same-asset NOI grew by 1.5%.业
data.description: Plaza Retail REIT revealed its financial results for the first half of 2025, with revenues reaching $31.8 million in the second quarter, up 3.6% from the same period in 2024. Net operating income increased to $19.1 million, a 3.8% rise from the previous year. The REIT's profit and total comprehensive income also surged to $12.7 million, compared to $2.4 million in the same period last year. Additionally, the REIT accomplished notable milestones, including property conversions and ownership expansions, improving its committed occupancy to 98%. Plaza Retail REIT increased its per unit FFO by 5.3%, achieved a 1.5% growth in same-property NOI, and registered a 14.8% blended leasing spread. The REIT's profit and total comprehensive income for the six months ended June 30, 2025, reached $22.0 million, up from $11.9 million in the prior year. These positive financial results were supported by increased revenue and operational efficiencies across their portfolio. Plaza Retail REIT remains focused on strategic enhancements and developments within its properties to drive long-term value for shareholders.
